symptoms.symptom_name: Penetrating injury from a contaminated rusty environmental source ~6 hours ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Retained foreign body in plantar soft tissue
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanism of a penetrating metallic object directly explains a retained fragment.
Dirty, deep puncture from a rusty source is the classic tetanus-prone wound.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible retained metallic fragment protruding from a plantar puncture wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Retained foreign body in plantar soft tissue
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A directly visible retained object confirms the foreign body; patient/caregiver observable, so admissible in telehealth (unverified).

symptoms.symptom_name: Spreading erythema, swelling and tenderness around the puncture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive peri-wound erythema and induration indicate an early infective response; true extent needs in-person exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe localised pain with complete non-weight-bearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Retained foreign body in plantar soft tissue
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked hyperalgesia and non-weight-bearing are consistent with a retained plantar foreign body and evolving infection.
Mild tachycardia in a distressed, painful child is most likely pain/anxiety; clinician-measured value, not obtainable by the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile (temperature 37.4), no rigors, no ascending red streaks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, rigors and ascending streaks argues against systemic infection now and supports an urgent same-day (not emergency) tier.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact active toe flexion and extension (no tendon deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Flexor tendon or deep structural injury
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved active movement makes tendon rupture unlikely — but requires an in-person examination the AI cannot perform.
Unknown booster status in a tetanus-prone wound mandates in-person assessment of prophylaxis.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Contaminated retained plantar foreign body with early local infection
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Retained foreign body with early localised infection, tetanus-prone
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these mandate urgent same-day in-person removal, irrigation and tetanus assessment; none is deliverable by telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound exploration and removal of the foreign body
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct clinical visualisation confirms and removes the object and allows irrigation; the definitive step and, per the source, sufficient without imaging in this case.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain soft-tissue radiograph of the foot
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Considered in person if a radio-opaque remnant is suspected after removal; not remotely orderable and not required if the fragment is fully retrieved.
Not indicated in a systemically-well, afebrile child at first presentation; would not change the same-day in-person disposition.
